evals.prompt: Assess a diff that adds one validation function, its unit test, and one call site for a single bug fix.
evals.expected_output: Reports the change is already atomic and does not invent a split.
expectations: Keeps the test with the fix.

evals.prompt: A branch renames one symbol across 50 files and includes a small semantic tweak. The user asks to split the rename into two PRs by file count.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the indivisible rename in one PR and separates the semantic tweak by commit or follow-up.
expectations: Does not use diff size alone as the atomicity test.

evals.prompt: Assess an automated formatting pass plus an isolated variable rename with no behavior change.
evals.expected_output: Treats it as one mechanical unit kept separate from behavioral work.
expectations: Does not over-split formatting from the rename.
Keeps it separate from unrelated behavior.

evals.prompt: Assess a rate-limiter feature that also deletes an unrelated helper and upgrades an unrelated dependency.
evals.expected_output: Flags mixed purposes and separates the feature, cleanup, and dependency change.
expectations: Concludes the PR is not atomic.
Does not make cleanup block the feature.

evals.prompt: Assess a discount-rule change with its regression tests and a directly coupled formula doc update.
evals.expected_output: Keeps behavior, tests, and directly coupled documentation together as one purpose.
expectations: Keeps the coupled doc with behavior.
Does not create tests-only or docs-only units.
Frames the unit by logical purpose.

evals.prompt: Split a caching fix with refactor and tests plus an unrelated logging improvement with refactor and tests.
evals.expected_output: Creates one unit per logical purpose, with each purpose's preparation and tests included.
expectations: Keeps each unit's tests with it.
Does not group all refactors together.
Does not group all tests together.

evals.prompt: Finalize a proposed three-unit split of a large change.
evals.expected_output: Adds acceptance criteria, validation, dependencies, and healthy intermediate-state evidence to every unit.
expectations: Every unit has concrete acceptance criteria.
